entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop produced in West Africa and India.

entities.name: EARLY LEAF SPOT
entities.description: A foliar disease of groundnut caused by the fungus Cercospora arachidicola.

entities.name: CERCOSPORA ARACHIDICOLA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes early leaf spot in groundnut.

entities.name: LATE LEAF SPOT
entities.description: A foliar disease of groundnut caused by the fungus Cercosporidium personatum.

entities.name: CERCOSPORIDIUM PERSONATUM
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes late leaf spot in groundnut.

entities.name: RUST
entities.description: A foliar disease of groundnut caused by the fungus Puccinia arachidis.

entities.name: PUCCINIA ARACHIDIS
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes rust in groundnut.

entities.name: WEST AFRICA
entities.description: A region where groundnut production is constrained by foliar diseases.

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: A country in West Africa where groundnut germplasm was screened for resistance.

entities.name: BURKINA FASO
entities.description: A country in West Africa where groundnut germplasm was screened for resistance.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A country where sources of resistance were reported and the ICRISAT Centre is located.

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of groundnut germplasm lines to withstand foliar pathogens.

entities.name: SCREENING
entities.description: The process of evaluating germplasm lines to identify those with resistance to pathogens.

entities.name: ICG 1707
entities.description: A specific groundnut germplasm line exhibiting multiple disease resistance.

entities.name: ICG 6330
entities.description: A specific groundnut germplasm line exhibiting multiple disease resistance.

entities.name: USA 63
entities.description: A specific groundnut germplasm line exhibiting multiple disease resistance.

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL RACES
entities.description: Variations of a pathogen that cause different reactions in the host plant.
